title: Login password sent as SHA-256 hash without server challenge (pass-the-hash)
description: The client-side login() function sends the password as a SHA-256 hash: password = self.url!.password.sha256(). On the server side in receiveSendLogin (ServerController.swift:1798), the password hash is compared directly against the stored hash via user(withUsername: login, password: password). This means the SHA-256 hash IS the effective credential. If an attacker obtains the hash (e.g., via FINDING_A_003 where hashes are leaked to clients with list_accounts privilege), they can authenticate directly without knowing the plaintext password -- a classic pass-the-hash attack.
recommendation: Implement a challenge-response mechanism: the server sends a random nonce, the client computes HMAC(password_hash, nonce) and sends the result. The server performs the same computation and compares. This prevents pass-the-hash attacks because the nonce changes each time. Note: the ECDH key exchange already provides some protection when encryption is enabled, but the wired.send_login message itself uses a simple hash comparison.
